It prepares the student for employment in such industries as chemical, petroleum, electrochemical, biochemical, semiconductor, nuclear, aerospace, plastics, food processing, environmental control, or related industries.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Transfer applicants are expected to complete, at a minimum, coursework equivalent to Berkeley's: </strong></p><p><br></p><p class=\"ql-indent-1\">CHEMISTRY 1A + 1AL + 1B</p><p class=\"ql-indent-1\">MATH 51 + 52 + 53 + 54</p><p class=\"ql-indent-1\">PHYSICS 7A + 7B</p><p class=\"ql-indent-1\">READING & COMPOSITION A</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Coursework must be completed by the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley. </strong></p><p><br></p><p>IGETC/Cal-GETC is not required. Students who choose to complete the entire IGETC/Cal-GETC pattern by the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley may use IGETC/Cal-GETC to fulfill the Reading and Composition Requirement.</p><p><br></p><p>Lower division courses required for graduation (but not for admission) are also listed in this articulation agreement. Completion of those courses is strongly recommended in order to strengthen one's application. All major courses must be taken for a letter grade. High grades in major courses (B and A grades exclusively) are essential for applicants to be both competitive in the admissions process and to be adequately prepared to continue with junior year coursework at Berkeley.</p><p><br></p><p>The applicant's Personal Insight Questions (PIQs) are important in the admissions process. The PIQs are reviewed for evidence of the student's interest in the chosen field and a thoughtful match between the intended major and academic and career objectives.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>For more AP Biology exam satisfies BIOLOGY 1A & 1AL.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>MATHEMATICS </strong></p><p>You may also satisfy this requirement with a score/grade of:</p><p>3 or higher on the AP Calculus AB exam satisfies MATH 51;</p><p>3 or 4 on the AP Calculus BC exam satisfies MATH 51;</p><p>5 on the AP Calculus BC exam satisfies MATH 51 + 52.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>ORGANIC CHEMISTRY WITH LABORATORY </strong></p><p>CHEM 12A (organic chemistry) is required for the Chemical Engineering B.S. degree. CHEM 12B is not required for the Chemical Engineering major, but it is acceptable in satisfaction of the science elective for the open elective program.</p><p><br></p><p>Transfer students are encouraged to take the organic chemistry sequence at their institution.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Completion of Chem 3A + 3AL + 3B + 3BL, with a mean grade of B or better, will constitute satisfactory completion of Berkeley's Chem 12A + 12B.</strong></p><p><strong>Students not meeting this requirement may be required to take Chem 12A and/or Chem 12B at Berkeley.</strong></p><p><br></p><p><strong>PHYSICS 7C</strong> is not required for the Chemical Engineering major, but it is acceptable in satisfaction of the science elective for the open elective program.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>READING AND COMPOSITION REQUIREMENT </strong></p><p>Coursework equivalent to Berkeley's: Reading & Composition A; or entire IGETC/Cal-GETC pattern completed by the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>NOTE:</strong> You may also satisfy this requirement with a score/grade of: 4 or 5 on the AP exam in either English Language and Composition or English Literature and Composition.</p>","groupId":"fce46f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":3,"area":"General"}]
